daily_brief: Diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war intensified Thursday, with Tehran reviewing a 14-point US proposal that could establish a framework for ceasefire negotiations [BBC], [Globe and Mail]. President Trump expressed optimism that a deal was close, though he threatened to resume bombing if talks collapse [France 24]. The conflict's economic reverberations continued to shape global markets: Japanese stocks surged to historic highs on peace hopes [Nikkei Asia], while Shell reported elevated Q1 profits driven by oil prices that have fluctuated between $100-120 per barrel since the Strait of Hormuz closure [BBC]. France announced it is repositioning its aircraft carrier strike group toward the Red Sea for a potential Franco-British defensive mission in the strait, distinct from US military operations [RFI], [Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire showed signs of strain as Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs for the first time since the April agreement, targeting a Hezbollah commander [BBC]. In the Pacific, Solomon Islands Prime Minister Jeremiah Manele was ousted in a no-confidence vote after weeks of political turmoil [RNZ]. UK voters headed to polls in local elections seen as a critical test for Prime Minister Keir Starmer's struggling Labour government [France 24].

top_stories.headline: Iran Reviews US Peace Proposal as Trump Predicts War Will End 'Quickly'
bullets: Iran's foreign ministry confirmed it is reviewing a US proposal described as a 14-point memorandum that could establish a framework for nuclear negotiations, with Pakistan serving as mediator
Reported provisions include suspension of Iranian nuclear enrichment, lifting of sanctions, and restoring transit through the Strait of Hormuz, though many terms are contingent on a final agreement
President Trump told reporters Iran 'wants to make a deal' after 'very good talks,' but earlier threatened to resume bombing if Tehran rejects the proposal
Iranian parliamentary officials dismissed the proposal as a 'wish list,' while Pakistan's foreign minister said his country is working to convert the ceasefire into a permanent end to the war

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage emphasizing both diplomatic progress and Iranian skepticism

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on unresolved issues including nuclear program and Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Framed conflict as 'high risk, high reward' game for both sides, included expert analysis on Iran testing US risk tolerance

top_stories.headline: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier Strike Group Toward Strait of Hormuz
bullets: France is repositioning its nuclear-powered Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier and escort vessels south of the Suez Canal into the Red Sea
The deployment supports a proposed Franco-British defensive mission to restore maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, separate from US 'Project Freedom' operations
President Macron said the mission may help restore confidence among shipowners and insurers while remaining 'distinct from the parties at war'
The Strait of Hormuz closure has triggered what the International Energy Agency calls the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized European autonomy from US operations and defensive nature of mission

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted broader diplomatic context including Macron's call with Iranian president

top_stories.headline: Israel Strikes Beirut for First Time Since Hezbollah Ceasefire
bullets: Israeli air strike hit Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh) targeting a commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force, the first attack on the capital since the April 16 ceasefire
Prime Minister Netanyahu said he personally approved the strike; local media reported Hezbollah unit members were meeting when the attack occurred
Lebanese health ministry reports Israeli attacks have killed more than 120 people across Lebanon in the past week, including women and children
Israeli military continues to occupy a strip of southern Lebanon; both sides accuse each other of ceasefire violations

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided context on ongoing violations by both sides and Lebanese casualty figures

top_stories.headline: Shell Profits Rise to $6.92 Billion as Iran War Drives Oil Prices Higher
bullets: Shell reported Q1 2026 profits of $6.92 billion, up from $5.58 billion in the same period last year, exceeding analyst expectations
Oil prices have surged since the war began, with Brent crude peaking above $120 per barrel before settling around $101, compared to $73 before the conflict
The Strait of Hormuz closure has disrupted approximately 20% of global oil and liquid natural gas supplies
BP previously reported its Q1 profits more than doubled; both companies cited improved oil trading results

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward financial reporting with context on war's impact on energy markets

top_stories.headline: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Ousted in No-Confidence Vote
bullets: Prime Minister Jeremiah Manele was removed by parliament in a 26-22 vote after just over two years in power
The political crisis began March 15 when mass resignations from a key coalition party led to formation of a 28-member opposition bloc including 10 cabinet ministers
Manele had refused to convene parliament for weeks until an Appeal Court ruling last Friday forced the vote
Manele remains in office until formally removed by the Governor-General; parliament adjourned to organize election of new prime minister

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of political maneuvering and constitutional process
